Just now

--

If you’ve made it this far, you probably noticed something.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

This article wasn’t really about a database.
It was about engineering judgment.
Technology changes every year.
Engineering principles rarely do.
The teams that consistently build reliable systems aren’t the ones chasing every new framework.
They’re the ones that know where each tool belongs.
Why Teams End Up With Bad Architecture
I’ve seen this happen over and over.
A startup begins with one PostgreSQL database.
Everything feels fast.
A year later the company grows.
Someone needs dashboards.
Another team needs AI evaluation metrics.
Marketing wants cohort analysis.
Finance wants revenue reports.
Support wants customer health scores.
Instead of introducing a proper analytical layer, everyone keeps adding more SQL to the production database.
Soon you have:

• Materialized views

• Cron jobs
